_purpose: M8-B2 rootless-podman golden fixture (task_08ec). This is the EXACT argv Magpie's DEFAULT (rootless podman + crun) review-container launch ships, as returned by reviewer.ts's buildReviewDockerArgs() when the runtime binary is `podman`. It is the M8-B1 docker floor golden (__fixtures__/reviewer-crun-floor-argv.golden.json) PLUS the single rootless-podman uid-mapping shim `--userns=keep-id` inserted immediately after `--user <uid>:<gid>` — every hardened flag is byte-for-byte identical. reviewer-podman-argv.test.ts asserts the built podman argv equals `argv` below byte-for-byte (order included). If this fails, either your change introduced an unintentional drift in the shipped-default posture (fix the code) or you deliberately changed it (update `argv` below IN THE SAME PR so the diff is visible in review).
_relationshipToFloorGolden: The M8-B1 floor golden pins the docker-runtime argv (no keep-id) and stays UNCHANGED by M8-B2 (its fixed test config uses dockerBin:'docker'). This fixture pins the podman-runtime argv. --userns=keep-id is a rootless uid-mapping shim (maps the invoking user's uid straight through so the container writes /out/findings.json back owned by the orchestrator), NOT a hardening flag; the hardened flag SET, VALUES, and ORDER are identical between the two goldens.
_normalizedInputs: argv below was built from FIXED, non-production placeholder values for every per-job/per-host variable (container name, uid:gid, /work, /out, /run/gw host paths) — see reviewer-podman-argv.test.ts's GOLDEN_INPUT constant. Only the security-relevant flag SET/ORDER and deployment-wide config values (image, memory/cpu/pids caps, gateway base URL, model) are meaningful here.
